title: Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window

compliance.assurance_rationale: HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(3)(ii)(C) — Reviewed: SPL provides contributing evidence for this regulatory requirement. The search query directly addresses the monitoring objective specified by the clause.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that HIPAA Security §164.308(a)(3)(ii)(C) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.5: Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.5' running on sourcetype epic:audit and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

compliance.assurance_rationale: NIST 800-53 Rev. 5 PS-4 Personnel Termination — UC directly tracks access-revocation within policy window on termination, matching the control's explicit workflow requirement.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST 800-53 PS-4 (Personnel termination) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.10.5: Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.10.5' running on sourcetype epic:audit and supporting catalogue-defined data sources, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757)
spl:
index=hr sourcetype="workday:termination" earliest=-30d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| table samaccountname, term_epoch
| rename samaccountname as user
| join type=inner user [
    search index=windows EventCode="4624" earliest=-30d
    | eval user=mvindex(Security_ID,0)
    | rex field=user "\\\\(?<sam>[^\\\\]+)$"
    | eval user=coalesce(sam,user)
    | table _time, user, WorkstationName, Logon_Type ]
| where _time > term_epoch
| eval hours_after_term=round((_time-term_epoch)/3600,2)
description: Detects logons, VPN sessions, or EHR access after HR termination timestamp so access revocation SLAs are provably enforced.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for HIPAA Security, NIST 800-53 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes citrix, epic, hr and sourcetypes epic:audit`, workday:termination. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Ingest authoritative termination feed within 15 minutes SLA; (2) tune out break-glass shared accounts; (3) auto-disable via SOAR optional; (4) weekly HIPAA access committee digest. Indexes required: `index=citrix`, `index=epic`, `index=hr`, `index=windows`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=epic:audit``, `sourcetype=workday:termination`.
visualization: Table (user, hours_after_term, workstation), Timeline (violations), Single value (count).
knownFalsePositives: PHI access from authorized clinical workflows, billing operations, audit reviews, or BAA partners.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624, `index=citrix`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=hr` terminations (`term_date`, `samaccountname`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Ingest authoritative termination feed within 15 minutes SLA; (2) tune out break-glass shared accounts; (3) auto-disable via SOAR optional; (4) weekly HIPAA access committee digest.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=hr sourcetype="workday:termination" earliest=-30d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| table samaccountname, term_epoch
| rename samaccountname as user
| join type=inner user [
    search index=windows EventCode="4624" earliest=-30d
    | eval user=mvindex(Security_ID,0)
    | rex field=user "\\\\(?<sam>[^\\\\]+)$"
    | eval user=coalesce(sam,user)
    | table _time, user, WorkstationName, Logon_Type ]
| where _time > term_epoch
| eval hours_after_term=round((_time-term_epoch)/3600,2)
```

Understanding this SPL

**Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window (§164.308(a)(3)(ii)(C))** — Detects logons, VPN sessions, or EHR access after HR termination timestamp so access revocation SLAs are provably enforced.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624, `index=citrix`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=hr` terminations (`term_date`, `samaccountname`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: hr; **sourcetype**: workday:termination. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=hr, sourcetype="workday:termination", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **term_epoch** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

• Renames fields with `rename` for clarity or joins.
• Correlates data from a subsearch to enrich the primary result set with additional context.
• Filters the current rows with `where _time > term_epoch` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **hours_after_term** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window (§164.308(a)(3)(ii)(C))** — Detects logons, VPN sessions, or EHR access after HR termination timestamp so access revocation SLAs are provably enforced.

Documented **Data sources**: `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624, `index=citrix`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=hr` terminations (`term_date`, `samaccountname`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Authentication.Authentication` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.

Output fields: _time, Logon_Type, WorkstationName, ], hours_after_term, samaccountname, term_epoch, user.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the EHR audit trail (Epic, Cerner), walk known-good cases from the same workflow class, and confirm index coverage, field extractions, and access permissions match expectations.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (user, hours_after_term, workstation), Timeline (violations), Single value (count).
grandmaExplanation: We detect logons, VPN sessions, or EHR access after HR termination timestamp so access revocation SLAs are provably enforced. HIPAA is about keeping health records private and secure when we use them in care and operations.
